mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals a robust labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, thereby driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year correlation between monthly PAYEMS growth and subsequent 20-day forward returns is not significantly positive at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 25 basis points reveals an unanticipated monetary tightening that raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS month-over-month change exceeds 0.25 percentage points
failure_condition: More than 50% of triggered instances yield positive 5-day forward returns, or the mean return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When inflation remains above 3% year-over-year and the federal funds rate is above 4%, the combination of persistent price pressures and restrictive monetary policy squeezes corporate margins and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Median 20-day forward return following joint trigger is not negative, or the proportion of negative returns is not significantly greater than 50% (binomial test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is already below 4% (tight labor market), a further acceleration in payroll growth signals overheating that prompts the Federal Reserve to tighten policy more aggressively, which negatively impacts equity prices over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Average 60-day forward return in the regime is not negative, or the coefficient of the interaction term in a regression is not significantly negative at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP (chain-weighted) signals broad economic expansion that lifts corporate profits and investor sentiment, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 5-year correlation between quarterly GDPC1 growth and subsequent 60-day forward returns is not significantly positive at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals a sharp contraction in manufacturing activity that often precedes broader economic weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: More than 40% of triggered instances yield positive 20-day forward returns, or the mean return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in payrolls (above 200,000 monthly gain) and retail sales (above 0.5% monthly gain) signals a virtuous cycle of rising income and consumption, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change > 200,000 and RSAFS month-over-month change > 0.5%
failure_condition: Average 20-day forward return after joint trigger is not positive, or not significantly higher than the unconditional mean (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is already in restrictive territory above 4%, any month-over-month increase in the rate tightens financial conditions significantly, compressing equity valuations over the near term.
failure_condition: Forward returns following rate increases in this regime are not negative on average, or the proportion of negative returns is not significantly greater than 50% (binomial test p > 0.05).
